primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for hedge accounting designation in consolidated financial statements, specifically whether foreign exchange variations on such items can be designated as hedged items

primary_authority.reason: IFRS 9 governs hedge accounting and paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly states that only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with a party external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items, with limited exceptions for intragroup monetary items involving foreign operations

IAS 21 governs recognition of foreign exchange differences, including the treatment of monetary items and exchange differences arising from intragroup transactions that cannot be eliminated on consolidation

supporting_authority.reason: IFRIC 16 addresses net investment hedges in foreign operations and clarifies the assessment of hedge effectiveness for cross-border hedging instruments within a group
IAS 7 addresses classification of cash flows from changes in ownership interests in subsidiaries that do not result in loss of control, relevant to understanding equity transaction treatment

peripheral_authority.reason: IFRIC 17 addresses dividend payable measurement but does not address hedge eligibility or foreign exchange treatment of dividend receivables
IFRS 19 disclosure requirements for dividends and equity are topically related but do not govern hedge accounting eligibility
SIC 25 addresses tax consequences of changes in tax status, unrelated to hedge accounting for intragroup dividends

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements
Foreign exchange recognition treatment for intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary items ineligible for hedge designation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 generally prohibits designation of intragroup items as hedged items unless they qualify under the specific exception for foreign currency risk exposure in consolidated financial statements under IAS 21 paragraph 32

approaches.label: Net investment hedge for foreign operation monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 32 and IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provide an exception allowing foreign currency risk of certain intragroup monetary items (including dividend receivables in foreign operations) to qualify as hedged items in a net investment hedge if the foreign exchange differences affect consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: External party transactions eligible for hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 establishes that hedge accounting is available only for transactions with parties external to the reporting entity, which would not include intragroup dividends unless they qualify under the net investment hedge exception
